spec_match.rationale: Exercises the round <length> syntax directly with a radius slider against a chosen shape — closest to a 'spec sandbox' of the polygon cluster.

interactive.rationale: Shape buttons + radius slider + live preview + live CSS readout, all driving real clip-path changes.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Promises a shape picker and corner radius adjustment; delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Radius slider exposes the range but doesn't show the spec's per-vertex clamp (tan(angle/2)*segment/2) — a star at radius 50px renders identical to one at 100px because acute vertices clamp; the readout reports the unclamped slider value, which is misleading.

design_system.rationale: Support banner uses --accent-emerald / --accent-rose, control panels use --bg-paper/--border-black, no static-card, shape buttons override background and color together.

openQuestions.title: Display 'effective' clamped radius
openQuestions.detail: Show the value the renderer actually applied next to the slider value when clamping kicks in. A star at radius 80px probably renders with ~11px on acute vertices — authors should see that distinction.

openQuestions.title: Add a percentage-vs-length comparison
openQuestions.detail: The spec limits the rounding parameter to <length> (no <percentage>). Add a probe that attempts polygon(round 10%, ...) via CSS.supports and shows it fails to parse — teaches the type boundary.

openQuestions.title: Animate radius via the slider
openQuestions.detail: A small play button that animates radius from 0 to max and back reinforces that polygon(round) is animatable (the headline of the sibling animated-clip-path concept).

openQuestions.title: Pair with offset-path / shape-outside
openQuestions.detail: The shape-explorer name suggests a general explorer; today it's clip-path only. A toggle that applies the same polygon(round) value to offset-path and shape-outside would make this the all-contexts hub.

summary: Most spec-faithful concept in the polygon cluster — slider + shape picker against a live preview. Two real gaps: the slider value vs the renderer's clamped value is hidden, and the percentage-disallowed nuance is invisible.
